tasks.title: Godot + gdext Engine Spike
tasks.description: Build a mini-prototype in Godot 4 with gdext Rust bindings to evaluate the engine for RTS development.
tasks.details:
Dev A builds: 64x64 tile terrain with heightmap, 100 units with A* pathfinding, camera controls (pan/zoom/rotate), click-to-select and right-click-to-move, basic UDP packet send/receive between two instances.

Evaluate (score 1-5): Rust ergonomics, pathfinding perf at 200 units, terrain rendering ease, build iteration speed, documentation quality, minimap feasibility, basic UDP networking.

Ref: docs/research/engine-comparison.md
tasks.testStrategy: Prototype runs at 60fps with 200 units on 64x64 map. Camera controls are responsive. Units pathfind correctly. UDP packets transmit between two instances. Write evaluation report with scores.

subtasks.title: Set up Godot 4 project with gdext Rust bindings
Render 64x64 tile-based terrain from heightmap data
Spawn 100 units with A* pathfinding on tile grid
Basic UDP send/receive between two instances

tasks.title: Bevy Engine Spike
tasks.description: Build identical mini-prototype in Bevy to compare against Godot+gdext for RTS development.
tasks.details:
Dev B builds same prototype: 64x64 tile terrain, 100 units with A* pathfinding, camera controls, selection/commands, UDP networking. Same evaluation criteria as task 1.

Ref: docs/research/engine-comparison.md
tasks.testStrategy: Same criteria as Godot spike. Write evaluation report with identical scoring rubric for comparison.

subtasks.title: Set up Bevy project with ECS architecture
Basic UDP send/receive between two instances

tasks.title: Engine Decision & Workspace Setup
tasks.description: Compare both spikes, pick engine. Set up Rust workspace with game-core/game-client crates, CI pipeline, and project conventions.
tasks.details:
1. Compare evaluation reports side-by-side, decide on engine (if both marginal, Godot wins for editor/asset pipeline)
2. Create Rust workspace: game-core (pure Rust, zero engine deps), game-client (engine-specific), game-data (TOML configs)
3. GitHub Actions CI: cargo test, cargo clippy, cargo fmt --check
4. CONTRIBUTING.md with code conventions

Architecture: game-core is engine-agnostic. All game rules/stats/simulation live here. Engine layer is thin rendering/input shell.

Ref: docs/research/system-dependencies.md
tasks.testStrategy: CI passes on push. Both crates compile. game-core has zero engine dependencies in Cargo.toml.

subtasks.title: Create Rust workspace with Cargo.toml (game-core, game-client)
Create game-data/ with TOML config structure

tasks.title: Game-Core Foundation Types
tasks.description: Core data types in game-core: Map, Unit, Position, Entity, Player, World structs.
tasks.details:
Map struct: 2D tile grid, terrain type + walkability per tile. TilePosition (grid) and WorldPosition (continuous) coords. EntityId + PlayerId types. Unit struct: position, health, speed, state machine. World struct: central game state container (entities, map, players, tick counter).

Design decision: tile grid for buildings/resources, continuous coords for unit movement.

Ref: docs/research/system-dependencies.md — Foundation Layer
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for all types. World can spawn entities, query by ID, remove dead entities. Map correctly reports walkability.

subtasks.title: Map struct: 2D tile grid with terrain type and walkability
TilePosition and WorldPosition types with conversions
EntityId, PlayerId, Entity struct with components
Unit struct: position, health, speed, state machine
World struct: entities, map, players, tick counter
Unit tests for all core types

tasks.title: A* Pathfinding System
tasks.description: A* pathfinding on tile grid in game-core with movement costs and performance for 200+ units.
tasks.details:
A* on tile grid with configurable heuristic. Movement cost per tile type (normal=1, slow=2, impassable=inf). Path caching for repeated requests. Must handle 200+ concurrent pathfinding requests at 60fps (under 16ms per frame total).

Consider using `pathfinding` crate or rolling own for determinism control.
tasks.testStrategy: Benchmark: 200 concurrent paths on 64x64 grid under 16ms. Unit tests: shortest path correctness, obstacle avoidance, no-path-exists returns None.

subtasks.title: Implement A* algorithm on tile grid

tasks.title: Terrain Renderer & Camera
tasks.description: Render flat tile terrain (64x64) with camera controls: pan, zoom, rotate.
tasks.details: Create terrain mesh/sprites from game-core Map data. Tiles colored by type (grass=green, water=blue, rock=grey). Camera: WASD + edge scroll for pan, scroll wheel for zoom (min/max bounds), middle mouse for rotate.
tasks.testStrategy: Terrain renders correctly with colored tiles. Camera responds to all inputs smoothly. No rendering artifacts at zoom extremes.

tasks.title: Unit Selection & Commands
tasks.description: Click-to-select, box/drag select, right-click-to-move. Units rendered as colored capsules.
tasks.details: Render units as colored capsules (blue=male, pink=female). Single-click select with highlight ring. Box/drag select for groups. Right-click terrain to move (issues pathfind command). Selected unit info panel showing health and state.
tasks.testStrategy: Single and multi-select work. Right-click moves units along correct path. Selection indicator visible. Info panel updates.

tasks.title: Unit State Machine & Movement
tasks.description: State machine (Idle/Moving/Gathering/Attacking) in game-core. Units follow A* paths with collision avoidance.
tasks.details:
State transitions: Idle→Moving (on move command), Moving→Idle (arrived), Idle→Gathering (on gather command), Idle→Attacking (on attack command). Units follow path waypoints at configured speed. Basic collision avoidance (push apart overlapping units).

Ref: docs/08-units.md — movement speeds
tasks.testStrategy: Tests: state transitions fire correctly, units arrive at destination, gathering/attacking states stub correctly, overlapping units push apart.

tasks.title: Resource System
tasks.description: 3 resource types (Wood, Stone, Food), resource nodes on map, gathering action with timers and carry capacity.
tasks.details:
Resource enum: Wood, Stone, Food. ResourceNode: type, amount, position on map. Gathering: unit walks to node, waits gather_time (wood=varies by tree size, stone=1s small/10s big, food=5s), picks up min(remaining, capacity), walks to nearest depot, deposits into player stockpile.

Carry capacity: 15 (male), 10 (female). Stone special: 1 small stone per trip = 20 stone resources.

Ref: docs/03-actions.md, docs/07-resources.md
tasks.testStrategy: Tests: full gather cycle (walk→gather→carry→deliver), capacity limits, resource depletion (node empty), nearest depot selection.

tasks.title: Supply System
tasks.description: Supply mechanics: food+water=supply auto-conversion, unit consumption (1/10s), health drain outside depot range, auto-heal in range.
tasks.details:
1 food + 1 water = 1 supply (automatic conversion). Units drain 1 supply/10s from player stockpile. Supply reserve per unit (male=10, female=7). Outside depot range: use reserve first, then lose health at consumption_rate*3. Auto-heal in range: 1 HP/10s.

Ref: docs/07-resources.md, docs/09-buildings.md — Supply Depot
tasks.testStrategy: Tests: conversion rate, consumption timing, reserve usage, health drain when starving, auto-heal restores HP.

tasks.title: HUD, Resource Display & Minimap
tasks.description: Resource counters HUD, resource nodes rendered on map as colored boxes, basic minimap.
tasks.details: Top HUD: Wood, Stone, Food, Supply, Population counters. Resource nodes: trees=green boxes, stone=grey, berries=red. Minimap: small colored pixel map (terrain + units + resources). Carrying indicator on gathering units.
tasks.testStrategy: HUD updates when resources change. Minimap shows correct unit/resource positions. Carrying indicator visible during gather.

subtasks.title: Render resource nodes as colored boxes

tasks.title: Building System
tasks.description: Building placement and construction: Home Base, Supply Depot, Farm with supply cover range and population cap.
tasks.details:
Building struct: type, health, armor, size, owner, construction progress. Home Base (8x8x3): train workers, 20 pop cap, acts as supply depot. Supply Depot (2x2x2): supply cover range, shared stockpile. Farm (10x10x1): 60 food/30s per worker, max 2 workers.

Construction: check resources, deduct immediately, build timer. Placement: must be in supply cover range of Home Base.

Ref: docs/09-buildings.md
tasks.testStrategy: Tests: placement validation (terrain, range, overlap), construction flow (resources deducted, timer, completion), supply coverage calculation, population cap enforcement.

subtasks.title: Home Base: train workers, pop cap, supply
Supply Depot: cover range, shared stockpile
Tests: placement, construction, coverage, pop cap

tasks.title: Building Placement UI & Fog of War
tasks.description: Placement ghost (valid/invalid), construction progress bar, tile-based fog of war per player.
tasks.details: Placement ghost: transparent building preview at cursor, green=valid, red=invalid (terrain/range/overlap checks). Progress bar above buildings under construction. Fog of war: tiles are unexplored (black), fogged (dim), or visible based on unit sight ranges. Supply range circle indicator when placing depot.
tasks.testStrategy: Ghost follows cursor correctly. Color changes based on validity. Fog reveals correctly based on unit positions. Supply range circle accurate.

tasks.title: Combat System
tasks.description: Melee and ranged combat: damage-armor=HP loss, projectile with travel time and accuracy grid, friendly fire, unit death.
tasks.details:
Melee: (damage - target_armor) HP loss per hit at attack_speed. Ranged: projectile at speed 20/s, accuracy grid (60% center, 10% each of 4 adjacent cells). If projectile hits another unit at impact location, that unit takes damage. Unit removed on death (health<=0). Attack-move: move toward target, attack when in range.

Ref: docs/02-core-mechanics.md — Ranged Weapon Attack Accuracy
tasks.testStrategy: Tests: melee damage = attack - armor, ranged accuracy distribution over 1000 attacks matches 60/10/10/10/10, friendly fire hits nearby unit, death removes entity, attack-move transitions correctly.

subtasks.title: Ranged attack: projectile with travel time

tasks.title: Unit Upgrades & Tech Tree (Phase 1 Subset)
tasks.description: Unit upgrade paths (Worker→Axes→Axes+Spear) and 4 initial tech tree upgrades researched at Home Base.
tasks.details:
Upgrades: Level 1→2.1 Axes (10 wood, 20 stone, 1 rope), Level 2.1→3.1 Axes+Spear (50 wood, 50 stone, 1 rope). Stat changes on upgrade per docs/08-units.md.

Tech tree (4 of 10): Stone Tool (enables unit upgrades), Stone Axe Upgrade (+20% axe damage), Rope (5 grass=1 rope auto-convert), Skinning (gather skin from animals).

Research: queue at Home Base, timer, apply effects globally when done.

Ref: docs/10-tech-tree.md, docs/08-units.md
tasks.testStrategy: Tests: upgrade prerequisites enforced, stats change correctly, tech research timer works, global effects apply to all units.

tasks.title: Combat Visuals & Placeholder Audio
tasks.description: Health bars, attack animations, projectile rendering, death effects, placeholder beep sounds.
tasks.details: Health bar above each unit (green→yellow→red). Melee: unit lunges. Ranged: small sphere projectile arc. Death: fade out. Placeholder sounds: beep variations for attack, gather, select, build, death.
tasks.testStrategy: Health bars update in real-time. Projectiles visible and travel correctly. Death animation plays. Sounds trigger on correct events.

tasks.title: Two-Player Hotseat & Game Setup
tasks.description: Hotseat mode: toggle players with separate fog of war. Game setup screen. Victory/defeat screens.
tasks.details: Player switching button toggles active player, swaps fog of war and camera position. Game setup: select map, pick starting positions. Victory screen on enemy Home Base destruction. Performance: 200 total units at 60fps.
tasks.testStrategy: Player switch toggles fog correctly. Both players can issue commands on their turn. Victory triggers correctly. 60fps maintained.

tasks.title: Player Ownership & Win Condition
tasks.description: Player ownership on all entities, win condition (destroy Home Base), loot system, starting conditions.
tasks.details:
PlayerId on all entities. Win: all enemy Home Bases destroyed. Loot: 80% of victim's resources to attacker on Home Base destruction. Starting conditions per player: 1 Home Base, 5 workers, 200 food, 100 wood, 100 stone.

Ref: docs/09-buildings.md — Home Base loot
tasks.testStrategy: Tests: ownership enforced (can't command enemy units), win triggers correctly, loot calculation accurate, starting resources spawn.

tasks.title: Basic AI Opponent
tasks.description: Simple AI: gather, build, train, upgrade, attack-move. Two difficulty levels.
tasks.details:
AI decision loop every N ticks: evaluate state, choose action. Phases: gather (assign idle workers to nearest resources), build (supply depot, farm when affordable), train (workers up to pop cap), upgrade (research tech, upgrade workers to fighters), attack (attack-move all fighters toward enemy base).

Difficulty: Easy=0.5x decision speed, Normal=1x.

This is the FIRST REAL PLAYTEST MILESTONE — single player vs AI validates the core game loop.
tasks.testStrategy: AI plays a complete game without crashing. AI gathers resources, builds buildings, trains/upgrades units, and attacks. Easy AI is beatable by a new player. Normal AI provides a challenge.

subtasks.title: AI decision loop (eval state, choose action)
AI gather: assign workers to resources
AI build: place depot and farm
AI train: workers up to pop cap
AI upgrade: research and upgrade units
AI attack: attack-move toward enemy base

tasks.title: Save/Load & Game Speed Controls
tasks.description: Serialize game state for save/load. Pause, 1x, 2x speed controls. Performance profiling.
tasks.details: Serialize World struct to JSON or bincode (save). Deserialize to restore (load). Game speed: pause (0x), normal (1x), fast (2x) via keyboard shortcuts. Profile performance to ensure 200 units at 60fps, identify and fix bottlenecks.
tasks.testStrategy: Save game, load game, verify state matches. Speed controls change simulation rate. 200 units maintain 60fps.

tasks.title: Data-Driven Stats (TOML Config)
tasks.description: Move all hardcoded stats to TOML files. Game-core loads config at startup. Validate against design doc.
tasks.details:
Create TOML files in game-data/: units.toml (all stats from docs/08-units.md for male/female at all 5 levels), buildings.toml (from docs/09-buildings.md), resources.toml (gather times, amounts), tech_tree.toml (costs, prerequisites, effects from docs/10-tech-tree.md).

Config loader reads at startup. Both devs can tweak balance without recompiling.
tasks.testStrategy: All game values match design doc. Changing TOML values changes in-game behavior without recompile. Missing/invalid config values produce clear error messages.

subtasks.title: Validate all values against design doc
